description: FFI function receives a potentially null pointer
rationale: C functions often cannot handle null pointers; passing one causes undefined behavior or crashes
pattern: FFICall(fn, arg) where arg: *T and nullable(arg)
fix_template: Check for null before the FFI call: `? ptr.is_null() { ret Err(...) }`
alternatives: Use NonNull<T> for non-nullable pointer types

description: Function signature does not match the expected C ABI
rationale: ABI mismatches cause stack corruption, wrong values, or crashes at the FFI boundary
pattern: extern 'C' fn f(args) where args_layout != C_layout
fix_template: Ensure the function uses C-compatible types (i32, f64, *T) and correct calling convention
alternatives: Use bindgen to auto-generate correct signatures

description: MAGE string passed to C function without null termination
rationale: C strings require null termination; MAGE strings do not. Passing a MAGE string directly causes buffer overreads
pattern: FFICall(fn, s) where s: str and !CString(s)
fix_template: Convert to CString first: `v cs = CString.new(s)?; fn(cs.as_ptr())`
alternatives: Use a pre-allocated buffer with null terminator

description: Buffer passed to FFI function may not be fully initialized
rationale: Reading uninitialized memory is undefined behavior; buffers passed to C functions for writing must be properly sized
pattern: v buf = [0u8; N]; FFICall(fn, buf.as_mut_ptr(), N) where N runtime-variable
fix_template: Zero-initialize the buffer: `v buf = [0u8; N]` and validate the size

description: Ownership of heap-allocated memory transferred across FFI boundary without matching deallocation
rationale: Memory allocated on one side of the FFI boundary must be freed on the same side (same allocator)
pattern: FFICall(fn, box_ptr) where box_ptr from Box::into_raw()
fix_template: Ensure matching deallocation: `v ptr = ^T.into_raw(val); ... v val = ^T.from_raw(ptr)`
alternatives: Document ownership transfer in the FFI contract

description: Struct passed across FFI boundary lacks #[repr(C)] layout guarantee
rationale: Without repr(C), the compiler may reorder fields; C code expects a specific layout
alternatives: Manually marshal the data to a C-compatible layout

description: GPU buffers must be initialized before kernel dispatch
rationale: Dispatching a GPU kernel on uninitialized buffer memory causes undefined behavior and GPU hangs
fix_template: Initialize the buffer before dispatch: `buffer.fill(0)` or `buffer.copy_from(data)`
alternatives: Use a staging buffer with pre-filled data
